diet.en: Carnivorous. Preys on almost anything near water: fish, wildebeest, zebra, buffalo, even lion cubs and elephant calves. One of the strongest bite forces in the animal kingdom

habitat.zh: 半水栖，栖息于河流、湖泊、三角洲和沼泽。喜欢在岸边晒太阳调节体温。需要极大型专业围栏，仅适合动物园
habitat.en: Semi-aquatic; inhabits rivers, lakes, deltas and swamps. Basks on banks to regulate temperature. Requires very large professional enclosures; zoos only

appearance.zh: 体型巨大，灰绿色至深棕色。吻部修长，牙齿外露（闭嘴时第四颗下颚齿可见，区别于短吻鳄）。腹部颜色较浅，鳞片规则整齐
appearance.en: Massive body, gray-green to dark brown. Long narrow snout with exposed teeth — the 4th lower jaw tooth visible when mouth is closed, distinguishing it from alligators. Pale underside with regular, even scales

history.zh: 古埃及人将尼罗鳄视为神明，索贝克（Sobek）是鳄鱼头的神祇。古埃及人曾人工饲养尼罗鳄并给它们佩戴黄金首饰。现代分布于撒哈拉以南非洲，种群相对稳定，约25-50万条
history.en: Ancient Egyptians worshipped the Nile crocodile as divine — Sobek was the crocodile-headed god. Egyptians once kept tame Nile crocodiles adorned with gold jewelry. Modern populations across sub-Saharan Africa number around 250,000-500,000

funFact.zh: 尼罗鳄的牙齿一生中会更换50次以上！每颗牙齿下方都有备用牙，一颗脱落立刻长出新的——终身「自带牙科诊所」🦷 如果人类也有这个能力就不用看牙医了
funFact.en: Nile crocodiles replace their teeth 50+ times in a lifetime! Every tooth has a replacement tooth beneath it — as soon as one falls out, a new one grows in. They carry their own lifetime dental plan 🦷 Humans could really use that ability

myths.zh: 「鳄鱼流眼泪是因为后悔」——「鳄鱼的眼泪」其实是生理反应：进食时眼泪腺会分泌液体润滑眼睛，与情感无关。这正是英语「crocodile tears」（假惺惺）成语的来源。
myths.en: 'Crocodiles cry because they feel remorse' — 'Crocodile tears' are a physiological reflex: tear glands lubricate the eyes during feeding. This is the origin of the English idiom 'crocodile tears' (insincere emotion).

careTips.zh:
1. ⚠️ 仅适合专业动物园和科研机构，严禁私人饲养
2. 需要大型水陆两用专业围栏，安全防护等级最高
3. 以大型整条鱼和畜禽为主食
4. 与专业野生动物兽医合作
5. 遵守CITES国际贸易规定
careTips.en:
1. ⚠️ For professional zoos and research institutions only — strictly prohibited for private keeping
2. Large water-land professional enclosure, highest security level required
3. Main diet of large whole fish and livestock
4. Work with specialized wildlife veterinarians
5. Comply with CITES international trade regulations

origin.zh: 东南亚至澳大利亚北部（印度、斯里兰卡、东南亚、巴布亚新几内亚、澳大利亚北部）
origin.en: Southeast Asia to northern Australia (India, Sri Lanka, Southeast Asia, Papua New Guinea, northern Australia)

size.zh: 超大型（雄性4.5-6米，最大记录6.3米，重达1000公斤以上）
size.en: Extra-large (males 4.5-6 m, record 6.3 m, over 1000 kg)

temperament.zh: 地球上现存最危险的爬行动物。极度凶猛、攻击性强，任何靠近其领地的生物都可能被视为猎物。绝对不适合私人饲养
temperament.en: The most dangerous living reptile on Earth. Extremely fierce and aggressive; any creature entering its territory may be treated as prey. Absolutely unsuitable for private keeping

diet.zh: 肉食性，顶级掠食者。几乎捕食任何动物：鱼、龟、水牛、鲨鱼，乃至其他鳄鱼。咬合力约3700磅/平方英寸，是地球上已测量的最强咬合力
diet.en: Carnivorous apex predator. Eats almost anything: fish, turtles, water buffalo, sharks, and even other crocodiles. Bite force ~3700 psi — the strongest measured bite force on Earth

habitat.zh: 唯一能在海水中长期生存的鳄鱼，可游泳跨越数百公里海洋。栖息于河口、沿海湿地、红树林。仅适合大型专业动物园
habitat.en: The only crocodilian that can survive long-term in saltwater, capable of swimming hundreds of km across open ocean. Inhabits estuaries, coastal wetlands, mangroves. Large professional zoos only

appearance.zh: 体型巨大，是世界最大的爬行动物。灰绿色至深棕色，皮肤粗糙。吻部宽大有力，成年雄性头部骨质隆起（骨嵴）随年龄加深。腹部鳞片光滑
appearance.en: Massive build — the world's largest living reptile. Gray-green to dark brown, rough skin. Powerful broad snout; adult males develop bony ridges that deepen with age. Smooth ventral scales

history.zh: 湾鳄是恐龙时代的幸存者，鳄鱼家族在地球已存在2亿年，几乎没有进化改变。澳大利亚原住民视其为祖先图腾，20世纪中期因皮革贸易几近灭绝，经过保护后种群恢复至约20万条
history.en: Saltwater crocodiles are survivors from the age of dinosaurs; the crocodilian family has existed on Earth for 200 million years with little evolutionary change. Revered as ancestral totems by Australian Aboriginal peoples; nearly hunted to extinction mid-20th century for leather; populations have recovered to ~200,000

funFact.zh: 湾鳄是游泳冠军！它们曾被卫星追踪到在开放海洋中游泳超过1000公里，横跨多个岛屿——一条鳄鱼，完成了人类需要飞机才能做到的旅行🐊 这也解释了它们为何分布如此广泛
funFact.en: Saltwater crocodiles are champion swimmers! Satellite tracking has recorded individuals swimming over 1,000 km across open ocean between islands — a crocodile completing a journey that would require a plane for humans 🐊 This explains their remarkably wide distribution

myths.zh: 「只要不靠近水边就是安全的」——湾鳄曾多次被记录到在陆地上追逐猎物，奔跑速度可达17公里/小时冲刺。它们也曾在海上袭击船只。保持安全距离意味着远离任何它们可能出现的区域。
myths.en: 'You're safe as long as you stay away from the water' — saltwater crocodiles have been recorded chasing prey on land at up to 17 km/h in bursts, and have attacked boats at sea. Safety means staying well away from any area they may inhabit.

careTips.zh:
1. ⚠️ 世界上最危险的爬行动物，严禁任何形式私人饲养
2. 仅适合具备最高安全标准的专业动物园
3. 需要超大型室内外复合专业围栏
4. 喂食采用全自动投喂系统，禁止人员接触
5. 遵守CITES及各国野生动物法规
careTips.en:
1. ⚠️ World's most dangerous reptile — strictly prohibited for any private keeping
2. Only suitable for professional zoos with the highest safety standards
3. Requires very large indoor/outdoor professional complex enclosure
4. Feeding via automated systems; no direct human contact
5. Comply with CITES and national wildlife regulations
